{{Quote|We were told not to use the scramjets during combat, but it was just too tempting at Mon Cal. It doesn't get much more exciting than using the scramjet to overtake one of those droid TIE fighters.|Anonymous Rogue Squadron [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]]|<ref name="RS I guide" />}}
The '''V-wing airspeeder''' was a combat [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeder]] developed by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] to replace aging [[Cloud car/Legends|cloud cars]] and allow more expensive [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] to focus on space combat.

==Characteristics==
The [[Verpine/Legends|Verpine]] [[Slayn & Korpil/Legends|Slayn & Korpil]] hive colonies, famous for their development of the [[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter/Legends|B-wing starfighter]], designed the V-wing [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeder]]. The airspeeder's standard [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] system was paired with efficient ion afterburners to provide the V-wing with fantastic speed.

The single-[[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] craft's top combat speed was 1,000 kilometers per hour. Additionally, when the powerful [[Chab-Ylwoum]] [[scramjet booster]] was employed, the craft's top speed was increased to 1,400 kilometers per hour. Braking and maneuvering flaps were at the rear of the craft. Heat dispersal fins were placed between the engines.<ref name="RS I guide" /> Unfortunately, combat at such a speed was potentially dangerous, and overuse of the scramjet could cause dangerous overheating of the drive system, so the scramjet was reserved for hasty retreats. The V-wing's lack of shields required the pilot to use the craft's size and speed against enemy speeders and starfighters.

[[File:Vwing_egvv.jpg|thumb|left|200px|V-wing [[:Category:Technical drawing images|schematics]].]]

The V-wing's weapon systems had two fire-linked [[Blaster cannon/Legends|blaster cannons]] on either side of the vehicle<ref name="RS I guide" /> with an effective range of two kilometers. The blaster cannons also had a rapid-fire setting that worked well against [[Deflector shield/Legends|shielded]] targets, but after approximately three seconds of continuous fire, the cannons would overheat.

Because of their unusually high flight ceiling, V-wings could be deployed from both ground-based hangars and from hangar-equipped starships. When deployed from space, the airspeeder relied on [[V-wing Speeder Transport, Model A|V-wing Speeder Transports]] which were capable of carrying up to four ([[V-wing Speeder Transport, Model B|later models]] carrying six) V-wings for transport into planetary [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmospheres]]. A large capital ship could carry up to ten of these transports, allowing dozens of V-wings to be rapidly deployed to a world under siege.<ref name="TNEGtVaV" /> The craft also had a refueling port, located on the starboard stern.<ref name="RS I guide" />

==History==
As the struggle with the remnants of the Empire continued, New Republic military acknowledged the need for a low cost and low maintenance vehicle for planetary defense. The V-wing, with its rapid deployment, high speed and low cost, was the solution.

[[File:Battle_of_Calamari_RS.jpg|thumb|right|250px|V-wings lead an assault against a World Devastator during the First Battle of Mon Calamari.]]
The V-wing saw its first combat action at the [[Battle of Calamari]] in [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]].<ref name="DE" /> [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] and his fellow pilots of [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] flew the craft and disabled three smaller [[World Devastator]]s.<ref name="RS I guide" />

The V-wing also saw action in the [[Battle of Coruscant (Yuuzhan Vong War)|Battle of Coruscant]] to the [[Yuuzhan Vong]]. The V-wings were highly effective against the incoming atmospheric craft but were simply overwhelmed through sheer numbers.<ref name="SBS">''[[The New Jedi Order: Star by Star]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The V-wing airspeeder first appeared in the ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire]]'' comic book.

The V-wing has a missile launcher with six [[Cluster missile/Legends|cluster missiles]] in the game [[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (video game)|''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron'']],<ref name="RS I">[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (video game)|''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron'']]</ref> but it is absent from the V-wing entries in any reference books.<ref name="TNEGtVaV" /><ref name="a&eg" /> It seems likely that the missile launcher is not considered [[canon]]ical, but is simply an artifact of game mechanics.

The design of the V-wing was recycled from an early design of the [[T-47 airspeeder/Legends|T-47 airspeeder]].
